message:
perf(func): avoid SOUNDEX text argument allocation

SoundexFunc previously called args[0].to_text() which allocates a fresh
String for every invocation even when the argument is already SQLite
text. Switch to the shared text_arg(&args[0]) helper, which returns a
Cow<str> borrowed from the underlying SmallText/Blob/etc., and pass
s.as_ref() into soundex(). The hot path no longer copies the input
string per row.

Also adds a perf-only ignored test (perf_soundex_text_arg) following the
same shape as the other text-arg microbenchmarks already in this file:
1M invocations of SOUNDEX("Robert") across 7 repeats, recording the best
elapsed wall-clock time. Tagged #[ignore = "perf-only benchmark"] so it
is skipped by `cargo test` and only runs when explicitly invoked.
